items.title: Open Source AI — Meta
items.author: ai.meta.com
items.snippet: Meta's most significant recent open-source AI release is the Llama 4 model family, announced as 'the beginning of a new era of natively multimodal AI innovation.' The Llama 4 herd includes Llama 4 Scout (17B active params, 16 experts), Llama 4 Maverick (17B active params, 128 experts), and Llama 4 Behemoth (288B active params). These are Meta's first open-weight natively multimodal models and first built on a mixture-of-experts (MoE) architecture. Maverick is benchmarked against GPT-4o and Gemin

items.title: Celebrating the first year of Copilot with significant new innovations - The Official Microsoft Blog
items.author: blogs.microsoft.com
items.snippet: Microsoft has been actively expanding its AI Copilot ecosystem in early 2026. In March 2026, Microsoft announced 'Wave 3' of Microsoft 365 Copilot, introducing expanded model diversity including Claude and next-generation OpenAI models, general availability of Agent 365 at $15/user, and the new Microsoft 365 E7 'Frontier Suite' at $99/user launching May 1. A new intelligence layer called 'Work IQ' was unveiled to help Copilot and agents understand how individuals work, with whom they collaborate

items.title: How OpenClaw Became GitHub's Most-Starred Project in 60 Days
items.author: www.lowtouch.ai
items.snippet: As of late March 2026, the most dominant AI agent repository on GitHub is OpenClaw, which has surpassed 335,000 stars — eclipsing React's all-time cumulative star record — after going viral in late January 2026 and climbing from 9,000 to 60,000+ stars in just days. Created by PSPDFKit founder Peter Steinberger, OpenClaw leads every 'real-world agent' benchmark: persistence, local execution, messaging integration, and proactive scheduling. It has roughly 5x the stars of the second most popular pr

items.title: Cursor 2.3 is here! - Community Forum
items.author: forum.cursor.com
items.snippet: Cursor has been active with several major releases in March 2026. On March 25, Cursor launched support for self-hosted cloud agents that keep code, build outputs, and secrets entirely within a user's own network. On March 19, Composer 2 was released with frontier-level coding performance. On March 11, Cursor expanded its Marketplace with 30+ new plugins from partners including Atlassian, Datadog, GitLab, Glean, Hugging Face, and monday.com. Cursor also introduced Automations — always-on agents t

items.title: Startup Gimlet Labs is solving the AI inference bottleneck | TechCrunch
items.author: techcrunch.com
items.snippet: AI startup funding activity in early 2026 has been exceptional, with record-breaking rounds across the board. The largest headlines belong to OpenAI's $110 billion raise — one of the largest private funding rounds in history, backed by Amazon ($50B), Nvidia, and SoftBank at a $730 billion pre-money valuation — and xAI's $20 billion Series E announced in January. A 'human-centric' AI startup called Humans& (founded by Anthropic, xAI, and Google alumni) also made waves with a $480 million seed rou

items.title: GitHub Copilot for Jira — Public preview enhancements - GitHub Changelog
items.author: github.blog
items.snippet: GitHub has made several major AI agent and Copilot announcements over the past year. In May 2025, GitHub launched its Copilot Coding Agent, an autonomous agent embedded directly into GitHub that activates when a GitHub issue is assigned to it or prompted in VS Code — it pushes commits to a draft pull request and logs every step of its session. In October 2025, GitHub announced Agent HQ, a unified hub that brings coding agents from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Cognition, xAI, and others directly in

items.title: MineDojo/NitroGen: A Foundation Model for Generalist Gaming Agents · GitHub
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: The open source AI model community has seen significant activity heading into spring 2026. According to Hugging Face's State of Open Source report, the mean size of downloaded open models rose from 827M parameters in 2023 to 20.8B in 2025, reflecting rapid capability growth. China's ecosystem dominated open-source releases following DeepSeek R1's viral January 2025 debut, with Baidu alone going from zero Hub releases in 2024 to over 100 in 2025. items.author: huggingface.co

items.title: Open LLM Leaderboard — Hugging Face
items.author: huggingface.co
items.snippet: As of late March 2026, the open-source LLM landscape has reached near-parity with proprietary models in many domains. Top-performing open-weight models include GLM-5 (77.8% SWE-bench, 86.0% GPQA Diamond, 1,451 Arena Elo), Kimi K2.5 (99.0% HumanEval, 96.1% AIME 2025), DeepSeek-V3.2 (leading for reasoning and agentic workloads), and MiMo-V2-Flash (outperforming DeepSeek-V3.2 on software-engineering benchmarks at 1/2–1/3 the parameter count). A comprehensive LLM-Model-Comparison-2026 dataset on Hug

items.title: Council agrees position to streamline rules on Artificial Intelligence - Consilium
items.author: www.consilium.europa.eu
items.snippet: In the EU, the most significant 2026 AI policy development is the Digital Omnibus legislative package, which proposes to streamline and simplify the EU AI Act. On March 13, 2026, the EU Council agreed its negotiating position on the Omnibus VII package, which aims to delay high-risk AI system rules by up to 16 months — pushing deadlines to December 2027 for standalone systems and August 2028 for product-embedded systems. The IMCO and LIBE committees of the European Parliament adopted a joint rep
